messages.content: Who is Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt is an accomplished Assistant Professor at the Department of Data Science, University of the Punjab (PU), Lahore, Pakistan. He has over 33 years of experience in teaching and management, with expertise in cybersecurity, operating systems, and artificial intelligence.
Tell me about Dr. Arif Butt

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt is an Assistant Professor at Punjab University's Data Science department. He's a technology entrepreneur and cybersecurity expert with 33+ years of experience, combining academic excellence with industry innovation.
What is Arif butt's current position?
Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt currently serves as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Data Science, University of the Punjab, Lahore, Pakistan.
Where does Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt work?
Arif butt works at the Department of Data Science, University of the Punjab, Quaid-i-Azam (New) Campus, Lahore, Pakistan.
What is Arif butt's educational background?
Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt holds an MSc, MPhil (both with Gold Medals), and a PhD in Computer Science from PUCIT, University of the Punjab.
Tell me about Arif butt's degrees
Arif butt completed his PhD in CS from Punjab University in 2019, his MPhil in CS in 2009 (Gold Medal), and his MSc in CS in 2005 (Gold Medal), all from University of the Punjab.
What Gold Medals has Arif butt won?
Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt won Gold Medals in both his MSc and MPhil in Computer Science from the University of the Punjab.
What is Arif butt's research focus?

Arif butt's research focuses on fuzzy inference models applied to operating systems, embedded systems, and cloud-based services, particularly in decision-making under uncertain and imprecise conditions.
Tell me about Arif butt's research interests
His research interests include fuzzy inference models, operating systems, embedded systems, and cloud-based services, with emphasis on decision-making in uncertain conditions.
How many years of experience does Arif butt have?
Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt has over 33 years of experience in teaching and management, having served in both the Pakistan Army and University of the Punjab.
What is Arif butt's teaching experience?
With over 33 years of experience, Arif butt specializes in teaching embedded and real-time operating systems, system programming, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence.
What subjects does Arif butt teach?
Arif butt teaches embedded and real-time operating systems, system programming, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence at the University of the Punjab.
Tell me about Arif butt's cybersecurity expertise

Arif butt specializes in vulnerability research, binary exploitation, and exploit development, with deep understanding of software architectures, memory corruption techniques, and attack vectors for strengthening cybersecurity defenses.

Arif butt is a technology entrepreneur, serving as the Founder of Excaliat and Falcon-Hunt, and Co-Founder of Tbox Solutionz.

Tell me about Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Generative and Agentic AI course covers the evolution from rule-based systems to modern LLMs, with hands-on training in closed-source models (GPT, Claude, Gemini) and open-source models (Llama, Qwen, Mistral).
What does the Generative and Agentic AI course at Punjab University cover?

Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course includes AI history, DIKW pyramid, transformer architecture, prompt engineering, tokenization, embeddings, vector databases, RAG implementation, fine-tuning techniques, and OWASP Top 10 LLM vulnerabilities.
What topics are included in Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course?

The Generative and Agentic AI course by Dr. Arif covers machine learning renaissance (1990-2000), deep learning revolution (2006-2017), Google's Transformer Era (2017), emergence of LLMs (2019-2023), and rise of Agentic AI (2023 onwards).
What is the historical perspective taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
In Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course, the historical perspective covers the umbrella of AI, DIKW pyramid, and key breakthroughs from rule-based systems to large language models.
What applications of GenAI are covered in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers GenAI applications in computing, gaming, education, architecture, civil engineering, textile, agriculture, power, retail, e-commerce, finance, and healthcare industries.
How does Generative and Agentic AI course address cybersecurity?
In Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course, students learn how AI has expanded the cybersecurity attack surface and explore the OWASP Top 10 LLM app vulnerabilities.
What closed-source models are taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers closed-source models including OpenAI's GPT family, Anthropic's Claude, Google's Gemini, xAI's Grok, Perplexity, and Cohere.
What open-source models are covered in Dr. Arif's AI course?
The Generative and Agentic AI course by Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt includes open-source models like Meta's Llama, Microsoft Phi, Alibaba's Qwen, Deepseek, Mistral, and Mixtral.
How does Dr Arif teach accessing closed-source AI models?
In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif teaches accessing closed-source models via provider's API keys and their SDKs, with hands-on exercises for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models.
How are open-source models accessed in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course teaches accessing open-source AI models locally via Ollama, through Hugging Face transformers library, and using cloud-based inference endpoints.
What fundamental concepts are taught in Dr. Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers prompt engineering fundamentals, tokenization techniques, embeddings, and discusses SQL, No-SQL, and Vector databases.
What is Retrieval Augmented Generation in Dr Arif's course?
In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif teaches Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Agentic AI protocols like MCP (Model Context Protocol) and A2A.
What neural network architectures are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course covers the progress from ANN, CNN, RNN, LSTM to Transformer architecture from a researcher's perspective.
Does Dr. Arif teach how to build LLMs from scratch?
Yes, in Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches how to build an LLM from scratch and how to fine-tune AI models using Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning techniques.
What lab setup is required for Dr Arif's AI course?

For Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif guides students on setting up Jupyter Notebook environment, building virtual environments (uv, pip, conda), and using Cursor IDE and Google Antigravity IDE.
How does Dr Arif teach OpenAI GPT models in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif covers OpenAI GPT family, installing openai SDK, generating API keys, and accessing models using endpoints like models.list() and chat.completions.create().
What downstream tasks are practiced with GPT models in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on examples of GPT models for Question Answering, Classification, Text Generation, Code Generation, Translation, Summarization, and Named Entity Recognition.
How are Anthropic Claude models taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches Anthropic Claude models including installation of anthropic SDK, API key generation, and accessing models via messages.create() endpoint with streaming options.
What Google Gemini features are covered in Dr Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers Google Gemini models, installing google-generativeai SDK, using models.generate_content(), and accessing via OpenAI compatible API.
How does Dr. Arif teach prompt engineering in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr Arif teaches prompt engineering with hands-on examples of good vs bad prompts, zero-shot vs few-shot prompting, Chain of Thought (CoT), and Tree of Thought (ToT) reasoning.
What anti-hallucination techniques are taught in Dr Arif's course?
Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers anti-hallucination prompt engineering techniques and addresses prompt injection as the #1 LLM vulnerability of 2025.
Does Dr Arif teach Google Colab in his AI course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on training on Google Colab, its free and paid tiers, saving API keys in Colab environment, and accessing notebooks from GitHub and Google Drive.
What is covered about Hugging Face in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course provides a walk-through of Hugging Face Hub, creating accounts, generating secret keys, and understanding different types of HF models by architecture and training stage.
How does Dr Arif classify Hugging Face models in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif classifies HF models by architecture (Decoder-Only, Encoder-Only, Encoder-Decoder), by training stage (Base, Fine-tuned, Instruction-tuned), and by function (Preprocessing, Representation, Generation).
What are the ways to access Hugging Face models taught by Dr Arif?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's course teaches accessing HF models via cloud-based providers, locally using runtimes, using pipeline() API, AutoModel/AutoTokenizer, fine-tuning, and building models from scratch.
Does Dr. Arif teach about Hugging Face Datasets and Spaces?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers Hugging Face Datasets, Spaces, and the developer ecosystem including Transformers, PEFT, TRL, and Accelerate libraries.
How does Dr Arif teach accessing HF models via InferenceClient?
In his Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif demonstrates accessing open-source models with Hugging Face InferenceClient, OpenAI Chat Completions API router, and OpenAI Responses API router.
What is Groq and how is it used in Dr Arif's course?
Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers accessing open-source models via Groq service provider using Groq Chat Completions API and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
Does Dr. Arif teach local LLM runtimes in his course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers local LLM runtimes including Ollama and llama.cpp, with hands-on installation, model downloading, and access via GUI, CLI, and Python libraries.
How does Dr Arif teach programmatic access to local models?
Dr. Arif teaches programmatic access to Ollama models using Ollama's Native Python Library, OpenAI's Chat Completion API, and OpenAI's Responses API in his Generative and Agentic AI course.
What UI framework is taught in Dr Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes designing AI applications with user interface using Gradio, covering textbox, slider, dropdown, and file upload components.
How does Dr. Arif teach conversational AI agents?
In his course, Dr Arif covers conversational AI agents, stateless vs stateful bots, short-term and long-term memory, and building chatbots using OpenAI's Chat Completion and Responses API.
Does Dr Arif teach deployment on Hugging Face Spaces?

Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers creating Space repositories, designing and uploading AI apps including Network Traffic Classifier, Malware Detection, Phishing URL Classifier, and Code Vulnerability Scanner.
How does Dr. Arif teach the transformers.pipeline() method?
Dr Arif's course covers transformers.pipeline() with hands-on examples for sentiment analysis, text classification, text generation, NER, summarization, translation, and question answering.
What multi-modality topics are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches multi-modality including Image→Text (GPT-4 Vision, Claude, LLaVA), Text→Image (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion), Audio→Text (Whisper, Wav2Vec2), and Text→Speech (gTTS, Bark, SpeechT5).
How does Dr Arif teach tokenization in his AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers tokenization from string.split() and regex to modern BPE-based (GPT-2), WordPiece (BERT), and SentencePiece-based (Llama) tokenizers with hands-on practice.
What tokenization tools does Dr. Arif demonstrate in his course?
Dr Arif's course includes online tokenization tools like OpenAI Tokenizer and Tiktokenizer, plus hands-on with Hugging Face tokenizers for base and chat models.
How does Dr Arif teach vectorization in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif covers frequency-based (BoW, TF-IDF) and prediction-based vectorization including static embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe) and contextual embeddings (ELMo, BERT, GPT).
What embedding models are practiced in Dr Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small, Google's embedding-001, and Ollama's nomic-embed-text for generating embeddings.
Does Dr. Arif teach vector similarity measures?
Yes, Dr Arif's course covers dot product, cosine similarity, Euclidean distance, HNSW, and IVF with practical exercises on computing similarity scores and vector arithmetic.
How does Dr Arif explain Transformer architecture in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif provides deep dive into sinusoidal positional encoding, scaled dot-product self-attention, multi-head attention, residual connections, layer normalization, and feed-forward networks.
What attention mechanisms are covered in Dr Arif's AI course?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches self-attention, masked self-attention for autoregressive decoding, cross-attention for encoder-decoder interaction, and multi-head attention in his Generative and Agentic AI course.
Does Dr. Arif teach Mixture of Experts in his course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers Mixture of Experts (MoE) for scaling FFNs by routing tokens to specialized expert networks for efficiency and capacity.
How does Dr Arif teach loading OS models with Transformers?
Dr. Arif demonstrates downloading TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat using Transformers library, using AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer, and apply_chat_template() method in his course.
What inference methods are taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course covers inference using pipeline(), generate() method, and manual forward pass with AutoTokenizer and AutoModelForCausalLM for complete understanding.
How does Dr. Arif teach AI model selection?

Generative and Agentic AI course covers AI Benchmarks and LLM Leaderboards including Open LLM Leaderboard, Chatbot Arena, and factors like open vs closed source, model size, context window, and cost considerations.
What is the Chinchilla Scaling Law in Dr Arif's course?
Dr. Arif explains the Chinchilla Scaling Law in his Generative and Agentic AI course, teaching optimal model size and training data ratios for efficient LLM development.
Does Dr Arif teach tool calling in his AI course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers tool calling and function calling with hands-on examples using OpenAI's Responses API for web_search_preview, code_interpreter, and custom functions.
What custom function examples does Dr. Arif teach?
Dr Arif's course includes building Multiply tool with conversational agent, getting current date/time, weather information, and executing shell commands using Chat Completion API.
What is MCP protocol in Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches Model Context Protocol (MCP) including its architecture, primitives, client-server communication, transport mechanisms, and commonly used MCP servers.
How does Dr Arif demonstrate MCP servers in his course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on examples of using file-system MCP server, GitHub MCP server, Fetch MCP server, Manim MCP server, and Google Drive MCP server in Claude Desktop.
Does Dr. Arif teach building custom MCP servers?
Yes, Dr Arif's course covers MCP lifecycle, designing custom MCP servers, running in debugging mode with MCP Inspector, and production deployment with Claude Desktop.
What database topics are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif provides overview of modern database landscape including SQL, NoSQL and Vector databases, ACID vs BASE properties, and CAP theorem with practical applications.
How does Dr Arif teach SQL databases in his course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on practice with MySQL using Sequel Ace GUI, command line client, and Python mysql module for database operations.
What NoSQL database does Dr. Arif teach in his AI course?
Dr Arif teaches MongoDB including server setup, command line client (mongosh), GUI tool (mongodb-compass), indexing, and Python pymongo module with MongoDB Atlas.
How does Dr Arif teach vector databases in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif covers ChromaDB features, CRUD operations, custom embeddings, indexing techniques, and various chunking strategies including word-based, sentence-based, and semantic chunking.
What chunking techniques are taught in Dr Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes word-based chunking using textwrap, sentence-based using nltk.tokenize, paragraph-based, and semantic chunking methods with overlap strategies.
Does Dr. Arif teach LangChain in his course?
Yes, Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers LangChain Models, Prompts, Memory, and Chains components with hands-on examples for closed and open-source models.
How does Dr Arif teach LangChain memory types?
Dr. Arif demonstrates ChatMessageHistory for conversation history, window memory for recent messages, conversation summarization, and session-based memory for multiple conversations.
What document loaders are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif teaches TextLoader, Dox2txtLoader, PyPDFLoader, DirectoryLoader, WebBaseLoader, CSV/Excel loaders, SQL databases, and audio/video transcript loaders.
How does Dr. Arif teach text splitters in LangChain?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers CharacterTextSplitter, RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, TokenTextSplitter, and SemanticChunker with practical chunking exercises.
What retrievers does Dr Arif teach in his course?
Dr. Arif covers VectorStoreRetriever, WikipediaRetriever, and MultiQueryRetriever with hands-on implementation in LangChain.
How does Dr Arif teach RAG types in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches Naive RAG, Corrective RAG with web search validation, Adaptive RAG for complex queries, Agentic RAG with planning, and Multimodal RAG for cross-modal retrieval.
What is LangGraph in Dr. Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers LangGraph for graph-based workflows with nodes, edges, shared state, persistence, conditional routing, and dynamic workflows.
Does Dr Arif teach OpenAI Agent SDK?
Yes, Dr. Arif's course covers OpenAI Agents SDK with core abstractions (agents, tools, models, handoffs), agent lifecycle, tool invocation, state management, and multi-agent coordination.
How does Dr Arif teach fine-tuning in his course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers why fine-tuning small LMs is a 2025 trend, distinction between pretraining and fine-tuning, and spectrum from full fine-tuning to PEFT methods.
What fine-tuning approaches does Dr. Arif teach?
Dr Arif covers Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, QDoRA), Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT), and Direct Preference Optimization (DPO).

What hyperparameters does Dr. Arif teach for fine-tuning?

How does Dr Arif teach model evaluation in his course?

What alignment techniques are taught in Dr Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) as alternative to RLHF, eliminating reward model training by directly optimizing based on preference pairs.
Does Dr. Arif teach AI guardrails in his course?

What CTF challenges are in Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course includes CTF 1 through CTF 5 as capture the flag challenges associated with reverse engineering covered in HO 3.3.

HO 1.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers course information and protocols, CIA triad, threats to CIA, authentication vs authorization, cybersecurity categories, famous cyber-attacks, and course matrix.
What famous cyber-attacks are discussed in HO 1.1 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.1 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course discusses famous cyber-attacks including Morris, ILOVEYOU, Melissa virus, Stuxnet, Estonia attack, WannaCry, Lockbit, Pegasus, and DeepFakes.
What cybersecurity categories are covered in HO 1.1 of Dr Arif's course?

HO 1.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers categories including Information Security, Network Security, OS Security, Web-app Security, Mobile-app Security, Wi-Fi Security, Cloud Security, IoT Security, and LLM Security.
What virtual machines are installed in HO 1.2 of the Information Security course?

HO 1.2 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers using VirtualBox, UTM, VMware, Parallel Desktop, and MS Hyper-V for virtualization.
What OS concepts are recapped in HO 1.3 of Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
HO 1.3 in Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course covers versioning history of MS Windows, Linux, Mac OS, and Android, plus Linux file hierarchy, shell commands, and password storage.
What cryptography tools are covered in HO 1.3 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.3 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers Linux tools for encoding, hashing, encryption, digital signatures, and digital certificates.
What networking concepts are in HO 1.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

What services are practiced in HO 1.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 1.4 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course includes hands-on practice with telnet, ssh, ftp, MySQL, Apache web server, and ngrok for external access.
What is Wireshark used for in HO 1.4 of the Information Security course?

What is covered in HO 2.1 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers the six phases of pentesting, ethical hacking, vulnerability types, exploit and payload, OWASP Top10, and cyber-attack types.
What anonymity concepts are in HO 2.1 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.1 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers different ways to achieve anonymity and the difference between Deep web and Dark web.
What reconnaissance tools are covered in HO 2.2 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 2.2 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers passive reconnaissance tools including host, nslookup, dig, whois, knockpy, netdiscover, traceroute, whatweb, theHarvester, sherlock, wfw00f, Google Dorking, and OSINT framework.
What scanning tools are in HO 2.3 of Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.3 in Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course covers nmap, searchsploit, nessus, and OpenVAS for scanning services on Metasploitable2 machine.
What is CVE in HO 2.3 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.3 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers Common Vulnerability Exposures (CVE) as part of vulnerability analysis.
What is covered in HO 2.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Metasploit Framework, msfconsole anatomy, structure, and using auxiliary modules for scanning on Metasploitable2.
What services are exploited in HO 2.5 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 2.5 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers exploiting services on Metasploitable2 including information disclosure, bindshell, samba, vsftpd, httpd, tomcat, UnrealIRCd, distccd, VNC, SMTP, PostgreSQL, plus EternalBlue on Windows10.
What brute force attacks are in HO 2.5 of the Information Security course?

What types of payloads are generated in HO 2.6 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.6 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers generating payloads for new user creation, spawning local shell, bindshell, and reverse shell.
What privilege escalation techniques are in HO 2.7 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.7 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers common privilege escalation techniques and MSF post module for exploiting distccd and PostgreSQL on Metasploitable2.
What persistence mechanisms are covered in HO 2.8 of the Information Security course?

What covering tracks techniques are in HO 2.8 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?

What web app pentesting tools are covered in HO 2.9 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

What vulnerable web apps are used in HO 2.9 of the Information Security course?

What injection attacks are covered in HO 2.10 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?

What frameworks are in HO 2.10 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.10 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers Browser Exploitation Framework (BeEF) and Social Engineering Toolkit (SETOOLKIT).

What wireless attack tools are in HO 2.11 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.11 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers airmon-ng, airodump-ng, aireplay-ng for deauth attacks, and aircrack-ng, hashcat, John the Ripper for password cracking.
What Android development topics are in HO 2.12 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.12 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Android architecture, building Hello World app in Android Studio, app signing, and publishing on Google Play.
What Android pentesting tools are covered in HO 2.13 of the Information Security course?

Tell me about Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Generative and Agentic AI course covers the evolution from rule-based systems to modern LLMs, with hands-on training in closed-source models (GPT, Claude, Gemini) and open-source models (Llama, Qwen, Mistral).
What does the Generative and Agentic AI course at Punjab University cover?

Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course includes AI history, DIKW pyramid, transformer architecture, prompt engineering, tokenization, embeddings, vector databases, RAG implementation, fine-tuning techniques, and OWASP Top 10 LLM vulnerabilities.
What topics are included in Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course?

The Generative and Agentic AI course by Dr. Arif covers machine learning renaissance (1990-2000), deep learning revolution (2006-2017), Google's Transformer Era (2017), emergence of LLMs (2019-2023), and rise of Agentic AI (2023 onwards).
What is the historical perspective taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
In Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course, the historical perspective covers the umbrella of AI, DIKW pyramid, and key breakthroughs from rule-based systems to large language models.
What applications of GenAI are covered in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers GenAI applications in computing, gaming, education, architecture, civil engineering, textile, agriculture, power, retail, e-commerce, finance, and healthcare industries.
How does Generative and Agentic AI course address cybersecurity?
In Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course, students learn how AI has expanded the cybersecurity attack surface and explore the OWASP Top 10 LLM app vulnerabilities.
What closed-source models are taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers closed-source models including OpenAI's GPT family, Anthropic's Claude, Google's Gemini, xAI's Grok, Perplexity, and Cohere.
What open-source models are covered in Dr. Arif's AI course?
The Generative and Agentic AI course by Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt includes open-source models like Meta's Llama, Microsoft Phi, Alibaba's Qwen, Deepseek, Mistral, and Mixtral.
How does Dr Arif teach accessing closed-source AI models?
In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif teaches accessing closed-source models via provider's API keys and their SDKs, with hands-on exercises for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models.
How are open-source models accessed in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course teaches accessing open-source AI models locally via Ollama, through Hugging Face transformers library, and using cloud-based inference endpoints.
What fundamental concepts are taught in Dr. Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers prompt engineering fundamentals, tokenization techniques, embeddings, and discusses SQL, No-SQL, and Vector databases.
What is Retrieval Augmented Generation in Dr Arif's course?
In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif teaches Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Agentic AI protocols like MCP (Model Context Protocol) and A2A.
What neural network architectures are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course covers the progress from ANN, CNN, RNN, LSTM to Transformer architecture from a researcher's perspective.
Does Dr. Arif teach how to build LLMs from scratch?
Yes, in Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches how to build an LLM from scratch and how to fine-tune AI models using Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning techniques.
What lab setup is required for Dr Arif's AI course?

For Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif guides students on setting up Jupyter Notebook environment, building virtual environments (uv, pip, conda), and using Cursor IDE and Google Antigravity IDE.
How does Dr Arif teach OpenAI GPT models in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif covers OpenAI GPT family, installing openai SDK, generating API keys, and accessing models using endpoints like models.list() and chat.completions.create().
What downstream tasks are practiced with GPT models in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on examples of GPT models for Question Answering, Classification, Text Generation, Code Generation, Translation, Summarization, and Named Entity Recognition.
How are Anthropic Claude models taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches Anthropic Claude models including installation of anthropic SDK, API key generation, and accessing models via messages.create() endpoint with streaming options.
What Google Gemini features are covered in Dr Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers Google Gemini models, installing google-generativeai SDK, using models.generate_content(), and accessing via OpenAI compatible API.
How does Dr. Arif teach prompt engineering in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr Arif teaches prompt engineering with hands-on examples of good vs bad prompts, zero-shot vs few-shot prompting, Chain of Thought (CoT), and Tree of Thought (ToT) reasoning.
What anti-hallucination techniques are taught in Dr Arif's course?
Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers anti-hallucination prompt engineering techniques and addresses prompt injection as the #1 LLM vulnerability of 2025.
Does Dr Arif teach Google Colab in his AI course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on training on Google Colab, its free and paid tiers, saving API keys in Colab environment, and accessing notebooks from GitHub and Google Drive.
What is covered about Hugging Face in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course provides a walk-through of Hugging Face Hub, creating accounts, generating secret keys, and understanding different types of HF models by architecture and training stage.
How does Dr Arif classify Hugging Face models in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif classifies HF models by architecture (Decoder-Only, Encoder-Only, Encoder-Decoder), by training stage (Base, Fine-tuned, Instruction-tuned), and by function (Preprocessing, Representation, Generation).
What are the ways to access Hugging Face models taught by Dr Arif?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's course teaches accessing HF models via cloud-based providers, locally using runtimes, using pipeline() API, AutoModel/AutoTokenizer, fine-tuning, and building models from scratch.
Does Dr. Arif teach about Hugging Face Datasets and Spaces?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers Hugging Face Datasets, Spaces, and the developer ecosystem including Transformers, PEFT, TRL, and Accelerate libraries.
How does Dr Arif teach accessing HF models via InferenceClient?
In his Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif demonstrates accessing open-source models with Hugging Face InferenceClient, OpenAI Chat Completions API router, and OpenAI Responses API router.
What is Groq and how is it used in Dr Arif's course?
Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers accessing open-source models via Groq service provider using Groq Chat Completions API and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
Does Dr. Arif teach local LLM runtimes in his course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers local LLM runtimes including Ollama and llama.cpp, with hands-on installation, model downloading, and access via GUI, CLI, and Python libraries.
How does Dr Arif teach programmatic access to local models?
Dr. Arif teaches programmatic access to Ollama models using Ollama's Native Python Library, OpenAI's Chat Completion API, and OpenAI's Responses API in his Generative and Agentic AI course.
What UI framework is taught in Dr Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes designing AI applications with user interface using Gradio, covering textbox, slider, dropdown, and file upload components.
How does Dr. Arif teach conversational AI agents?
In his course, Dr Arif covers conversational AI agents, stateless vs stateful bots, short-term and long-term memory, and building chatbots using OpenAI's Chat Completion and Responses API.
Does Dr Arif teach deployment on Hugging Face Spaces?

Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers creating Space repositories, designing and uploading AI apps including Network Traffic Classifier, Malware Detection, Phishing URL Classifier, and Code Vulnerability Scanner.
How does Dr. Arif teach the transformers.pipeline() method?
Dr Arif's course covers transformers.pipeline() with hands-on examples for sentiment analysis, text classification, text generation, NER, summarization, translation, and question answering.
What multi-modality topics are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches multi-modality including Image→Text (GPT-4 Vision, Claude, LLaVA), Text→Image (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion), Audio→Text (Whisper, Wav2Vec2), and Text→Speech (gTTS, Bark, SpeechT5).
How does Dr Arif teach tokenization in his AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers tokenization from string.split() and regex to modern BPE-based (GPT-2), WordPiece (BERT), and SentencePiece-based (Llama) tokenizers with hands-on practice.
What tokenization tools does Dr. Arif demonstrate in his course?
Dr Arif's course includes online tokenization tools like OpenAI Tokenizer and Tiktokenizer, plus hands-on with Hugging Face tokenizers for base and chat models.
How does Dr Arif teach vectorization in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif covers frequency-based (BoW, TF-IDF) and prediction-based vectorization including static embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe) and contextual embeddings (ELMo, BERT, GPT).
What embedding models are practiced in Dr Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small, Google's embedding-001, and Ollama's nomic-embed-text for generating embeddings.
Does Dr. Arif teach vector similarity measures?
Yes, Dr Arif's course covers dot product, cosine similarity, Euclidean distance, HNSW, and IVF with practical exercises on computing similarity scores and vector arithmetic.
How does Dr Arif explain Transformer architecture in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif provides deep dive into sinusoidal positional encoding, scaled dot-product self-attention, multi-head attention, residual connections, layer normalization, and feed-forward networks.
What attention mechanisms are covered in Dr Arif's AI course?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches self-attention, masked self-attention for autoregressive decoding, cross-attention for encoder-decoder interaction, and multi-head attention in his Generative and Agentic AI course.
Does Dr. Arif teach Mixture of Experts in his course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers Mixture of Experts (MoE) for scaling FFNs by routing tokens to specialized expert networks for efficiency and capacity.
How does Dr Arif teach loading OS models with Transformers?
Dr. Arif demonstrates downloading TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat using Transformers library, using AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer, and apply_chat_template() method in his course.
What inference methods are taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course covers inference using pipeline(), generate() method, and manual forward pass with AutoTokenizer and AutoModelForCausalLM for complete understanding.
How does Dr. Arif teach AI model selection?

Generative and Agentic AI course covers AI Benchmarks and LLM Leaderboards including Open LLM Leaderboard, Chatbot Arena, and factors like open vs closed source, model size, context window, and cost considerations.
What is the Chinchilla Scaling Law in Dr Arif's course?
Dr. Arif explains the Chinchilla Scaling Law in his Generative and Agentic AI course, teaching optimal model size and training data ratios for efficient LLM development.
Does Dr Arif teach tool calling in his AI course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers tool calling and function calling with hands-on examples using OpenAI's Responses API for web_search_preview, code_interpreter, and custom functions.
What custom function examples does Dr. Arif teach?
Dr Arif's course includes building Multiply tool with conversational agent, getting current date/time, weather information, and executing shell commands using Chat Completion API.
What is MCP protocol in Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches Model Context Protocol (MCP) including its architecture, primitives, client-server communication, transport mechanisms, and commonly used MCP servers.
How does Dr Arif demonstrate MCP servers in his course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on examples of using file-system MCP server, GitHub MCP server, Fetch MCP server, Manim MCP server, and Google Drive MCP server in Claude Desktop.
Does Dr. Arif teach building custom MCP servers?
Yes, Dr Arif's course covers MCP lifecycle, designing custom MCP servers, running in debugging mode with MCP Inspector, and production deployment with Claude Desktop.
What database topics are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif provides overview of modern database landscape including SQL, NoSQL and Vector databases, ACID vs BASE properties, and CAP theorem with practical applications.
How does Dr Arif teach SQL databases in his course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on practice with MySQL using Sequel Ace GUI, command line client, and Python mysql module for database operations.
What NoSQL database does Dr. Arif teach in his AI course?
Dr Arif teaches MongoDB including server setup, command line client (mongosh), GUI tool (mongodb-compass), indexing, and Python pymongo module with MongoDB Atlas.
How does Dr Arif teach vector databases in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif covers ChromaDB features, CRUD operations, custom embeddings, indexing techniques, and various chunking strategies including word-based, sentence-based, and semantic chunking.
What chunking techniques are taught in Dr Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes word-based chunking using textwrap, sentence-based using nltk.tokenize, paragraph-based, and semantic chunking methods with overlap strategies.
Does Dr. Arif teach LangChain in his course?

What is covered in Module 3 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

What CTF challenges are in Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course includes CTF 1 through CTF 5 as capture the flag challenges associated with reverse engineering covered in HO 3.3.
What is the first handout in Dr Arif's Information Security course?
The first handout in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course is HO 1.1 covering course information, CIA triad, and famous cyber-attacks in Module 1.
What is the last handout in Module 2 of Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course?

What is covered in HO 1.1 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 1.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers course information and protocols, CIA triad, threats to CIA, authentication vs authorization, cybersecurity categories, famous cyber-attacks, and course matrix.
What famous cyber-attacks are discussed in HO 1.1 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.1 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course discusses famous cyber-attacks including Morris, ILOVEYOU, Melissa virus, Stuxnet, Estonia attack, WannaCry, Lockbit, Pegasus, and DeepFakes.
What cybersecurity categories are covered in HO 1.1 of Dr Arif's course?

HO 1.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers categories including Information Security, Network Security, OS Security, Web-app Security, Mobile-app Security, Wi-Fi Security, Cloud Security, IoT Security, and LLM Security.
What virtual machines are installed in HO 1.2 of the Information Security course?
HO 1.2 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers installing Kali Linux, Windows10, Ubuntu Server, Metasploitable2, Metasploitable3, and Flare-VM using hypervisors or Docker containers.
What hypervisors are used in HO 1.2 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.2 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers using VirtualBox, UTM, VMware, Parallel Desktop, and MS Hyper-V for virtualization.
What OS concepts are recapped in HO 1.3 of Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
HO 1.3 in Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course covers versioning history of MS Windows, Linux, Mac OS, and Android, plus Linux file hierarchy, shell commands, and password storage.
What cryptography tools are covered in HO 1.3 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.3 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers Linux tools for encoding, hashing, encryption, digital signatures, and digital certificates.
What networking concepts are in HO 1.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 1.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers TCP/IP stack, ifconfig, ping, arp, route, DNS tools, xinetd services, bind vs reverse shells, and firewall configuration with iptables, nftables, and ufw.
What services are practiced in HO 1.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 1.4 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course includes hands-on practice with telnet, ssh, ftp, MySQL, Apache web server, and ngrok for external access.
What is Wireshark used for in HO 1.4 of the Information Security course?
HO 1.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course uses Wireshark for capturing and analyzing network traffic while accessing various services in the virtual lab.
What is covered in HO 2.1 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers the six phases of pentesting, ethical hacking, vulnerability types, exploit and payload, OWASP Top10, and cyber-attack types.
What anonymity concepts are in HO 2.1 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.1 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers different ways to achieve anonymity and the difference between Deep web and Dark web.
What reconnaissance tools are covered in HO 2.2 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 2.2 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers passive reconnaissance tools including host, nslookup, dig, whois, knockpy, netdiscover, traceroute, whatweb, theHarvester, sherlock, wfw00f, Google Dorking, and OSINT framework.
What scanning tools are in HO 2.3 of Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.3 in Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course covers nmap, searchsploit, nessus, and OpenVAS for scanning services on Metasploitable2 machine.
What is CVE in HO 2.3 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.3 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers Common Vulnerability Exposures (CVE) as part of vulnerability analysis.
What is covered in HO 2.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Metasploit Framework, msfconsole anatomy, structure, and using auxiliary modules for scanning on Metasploitable2.
What services are exploited in HO 2.5 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 2.5 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers exploiting services on Metasploitable2 including information disclosure, bindshell, samba, vsftpd, httpd, tomcat, UnrealIRCd, distccd, VNC, SMTP, PostgreSQL, plus EternalBlue on Windows10.
What brute force attacks are in HO 2.5 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.5 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers launching brute force attacks on ssh and telnet services running on Metasploitable2.
What payload tools are covered in HO 2.6 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.6 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers msfvenom and veil for generating payloads, plus VirusTotal for analyzing malicious programs.
What types of payloads are generated in HO 2.6 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.6 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers generating payloads for new user creation, spawning local shell, bindshell, and reverse shell.
What privilege escalation techniques are in HO 2.7 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.7 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers common privilege escalation techniques and MSF post module for exploiting distccd and PostgreSQL on Metasploitable2.
What persistence mechanisms are covered in HO 2.8 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.8 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers maintaining access after exploiting NetBIOS on Metasploitable3 using EternalBlue exploit.
What covering tracks techniques are in HO 2.8 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.8 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers log files in Linux and Windows and common tools for clearing tracks.
What web app pentesting tools are covered in HO 2.9 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.9 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Burp suite (Proxy, Target, Repeater, Intruder), hydra, and DVWA for web app pentesting.
What vulnerable web apps are used in HO 2.9 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.9 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers DVWA, WebGoat, and Juice Shop as insecure web applications for practice.
What injection attacks are covered in HO 2.10 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.10 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers command injection, SQL injection using SQLMap, XSS, CSRF, and SSRF vulnerabilities.
What frameworks are in HO 2.10 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.10 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers Browser Exploitation Framework (BeEF) and Social Engineering Toolkit (SETOOLKIT).
What wireless security protocols are covered in HO 2.11 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.11 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers WEP, WPA, WPA2, and WPA3 security protocols and the 4-way handshake.
What wireless attack tools are in HO 2.11 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.11 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers airmon-ng, airodump-ng, aireplay-ng for deauth attacks, and aircrack-ng, hashcat, John the Ripper for password cracking.
What Android development topics are in HO 2.12 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.12 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Android architecture, building Hello World app in Android Studio, app signing, and publishing on Google Play.
What Android pentesting tools are covered in HO 2.13 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.13 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers Genymotion, ADB, apktool, jadx, dex2jar, and MobSF for Android reverse engineering and static analysis.

Tell me about Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Generative and Agentic AI course covers the evolution from rule-based systems to modern LLMs, with hands-on training in closed-source models (GPT, Claude, Gemini) and open-source models (Llama, Qwen, Mistral).
What does the Generative and Agentic AI course at Punjab University cover?

Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course includes AI history, DIKW pyramid, transformer architecture, prompt engineering, tokenization, embeddings, vector databases, RAG implementation, fine-tuning techniques, and OWASP Top 10 LLM vulnerabilities.
What topics are included in Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course?

The Generative and Agentic AI course by Dr. Arif covers machine learning renaissance (1990-2000), deep learning revolution (2006-2017), Google's Transformer Era (2017), emergence of LLMs (2019-2023), and rise of Agentic AI (2023 onwards).
What is the historical perspective taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
In Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course, the historical perspective covers the umbrella of AI, DIKW pyramid, and key breakthroughs from rule-based systems to large language models.
What applications of GenAI are covered in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers GenAI applications in computing, gaming, education, architecture, civil engineering, textile, agriculture, power, retail, e-commerce, finance, and healthcare industries.
How does Generative and Agentic AI course address cybersecurity?
In Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course, students learn how AI has expanded the cybersecurity attack surface and explore the OWASP Top 10 LLM app vulnerabilities.
What closed-source models are taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers closed-source models including OpenAI's GPT family, Anthropic's Claude, Google's Gemini, xAI's Grok, Perplexity, and Cohere.
What open-source models are covered in Dr. Arif's AI course?
The Generative and Agentic AI course by Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt includes open-source models like Meta's Llama, Microsoft Phi, Alibaba's Qwen, Deepseek, Mistral, and Mixtral.
How does Dr Arif teach accessing closed-source AI models?
In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif teaches accessing closed-source models via provider's API keys and their SDKs, with hands-on exercises for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models.
How are open-source models accessed in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course teaches accessing open-source AI models locally via Ollama, through Hugging Face transformers library, and using cloud-based inference endpoints.
What fundamental concepts are taught in Dr. Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers prompt engineering fundamentals, tokenization techniques, embeddings, and discusses SQL, No-SQL, and Vector databases.
What is Retrieval Augmented Generation in Dr Arif's course?
In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif teaches Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Agentic AI protocols like MCP (Model Context Protocol) and A2A.
What neural network architectures are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course covers the progress from ANN, CNN, RNN, LSTM to Transformer architecture from a researcher's perspective.
Does Dr. Arif teach how to build LLMs from scratch?
Yes, in Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches how to build an LLM from scratch and how to fine-tune AI models using Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning techniques.
What lab setup is required for Dr Arif's AI course?

For Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif guides students on setting up Jupyter Notebook environment, building virtual environments (uv, pip, conda), and using Cursor IDE and Google Antigravity IDE.
How does Dr Arif teach OpenAI GPT models in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif covers OpenAI GPT family, installing openai SDK, generating API keys, and accessing models using endpoints like models.list() and chat.completions.create().
What downstream tasks are practiced with GPT models in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on examples of GPT models for Question Answering, Classification, Text Generation, Code Generation, Translation, Summarization, and Named Entity Recognition.
How are Anthropic Claude models taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches Anthropic Claude models including installation of anthropic SDK, API key generation, and accessing models via messages.create() endpoint with streaming options.
What Google Gemini features are covered in Dr Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers Google Gemini models, installing google-generativeai SDK, using models.generate_content(), and accessing via OpenAI compatible API.
How does Dr. Arif teach prompt engineering in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr Arif teaches prompt engineering with hands-on examples of good vs bad prompts, zero-shot vs few-shot prompting, Chain of Thought (CoT), and Tree of Thought (ToT) reasoning.
What anti-hallucination techniques are taught in Dr Arif's course?
Dr. Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers anti-hallucination prompt engineering techniques and addresses prompt injection as the #1 LLM vulnerability of 2025.
Does Dr Arif teach Google Colab in his AI course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on training on Google Colab, its free and paid tiers, saving API keys in Colab environment, and accessing notebooks from GitHub and Google Drive.
What is covered about Hugging Face in Dr. Arif's course?

Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course provides a walk-through of Hugging Face Hub, creating accounts, generating secret keys, and understanding different types of HF models by architecture and training stage.
How does Dr Arif classify Hugging Face models in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif classifies HF models by architecture (Decoder-Only, Encoder-Only, Encoder-Decoder), by training stage (Base, Fine-tuned, Instruction-tuned), and by function (Preprocessing, Representation, Generation).
What are the ways to access Hugging Face models taught by Dr Arif?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's course teaches accessing HF models via cloud-based providers, locally using runtimes, using pipeline() API, AutoModel/AutoTokenizer, fine-tuning, and building models from scratch.
Does Dr. Arif teach about Hugging Face Datasets and Spaces?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers Hugging Face Datasets, Spaces, and the developer ecosystem including Transformers, PEFT, TRL, and Accelerate libraries.
How does Dr Arif teach accessing HF models via InferenceClient?
In his Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif demonstrates accessing open-source models with Hugging Face InferenceClient, OpenAI Chat Completions API router, and OpenAI Responses API router.
What is Groq and how is it used in Dr Arif's course?
Dr Arif's Generative and Agentic AI course covers accessing open-source models via Groq service provider using Groq Chat Completions API and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
Does Dr. Arif teach local LLM runtimes in his course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers local LLM runtimes including Ollama and llama.cpp, with hands-on installation, model downloading, and access via GUI, CLI, and Python libraries.
How does Dr Arif teach programmatic access to local models?
Dr. Arif teaches programmatic access to Ollama models using Ollama's Native Python Library, OpenAI's Chat Completion API, and OpenAI's Responses API in his Generative and Agentic AI course.
What UI framework is taught in Dr Arif's AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes designing AI applications with user interface using Gradio, covering textbox, slider, dropdown, and file upload components.
How does Dr. Arif teach conversational AI agents?
In his course, Dr Arif covers conversational AI agents, stateless vs stateful bots, short-term and long-term memory, and building chatbots using OpenAI's Chat Completion and Responses API.
Does Dr Arif teach deployment on Hugging Face Spaces?

Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers creating Space repositories, designing and uploading AI apps including Network Traffic Classifier, Malware Detection, Phishing URL Classifier, and Code Vulnerability Scanner.
How does Dr. Arif teach the transformers.pipeline() method?
Dr Arif's course covers transformers.pipeline() with hands-on examples for sentiment analysis, text classification, text generation, NER, summarization, translation, and question answering.
What multi-modality topics are covered in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif teaches multi-modality including Image→Text (GPT-4 Vision, Claude, LLaVA), Text→Image (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion), Audio→Text (Whisper, Wav2Vec2), and Text→Speech (gTTS, Bark, SpeechT5).
How does Dr Arif teach tokenization in his AI course?
Generative and Agentic AI course covers tokenization from string.split() and regex to modern BPE-based (GPT-2), WordPiece (BERT), and SentencePiece-based (Llama) tokenizers with hands-on practice.
What tokenization tools does Dr. Arif demonstrate in his course?
Dr Arif's course includes online tokenization tools like OpenAI Tokenizer and Tiktokenizer, plus hands-on with Hugging Face tokenizers for base and chat models.
How does Dr Arif teach vectorization in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr. Arif covers frequency-based (BoW, TF-IDF) and prediction-based vectorization including static embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe) and contextual embeddings (ELMo, BERT, GPT).
What embedding models are practiced in Dr Arif's course?
Generative and Agentic AI course includes hands-on with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small, Google's embedding-001, and Ollama's nomic-embed-text for generating embeddings.
Does Dr. Arif teach vector similarity measures?
Yes, Dr Arif's course covers dot product, cosine similarity, Euclidean distance, HNSW, and IVF with practical exercises on computing similarity scores and vector arithmetic.
How does Dr Arif explain Transformer architecture in his course?

In Generative and Agentic AI course, Dr. Arif provides deep dive into sinusoidal positional encoding, scaled dot-product self-attention, multi-head attention, residual connections, layer normalization, and feed-forward networks.
What attention mechanisms are covered in Dr Arif's AI course?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt teaches self-attention, masked self-attention for autoregressive decoding, cross-attention for encoder-decoder interaction, and multi-head attention in his Generative and Agentic AI course.
Does Dr. Arif teach Mixture of Experts in his course?
Yes, Generative and Agentic AI course covers Mixture of Experts (MoE) for scaling FFNs by routing tokens to specialized expert networks for efficiency and capacity.
How does Dr Arif teach loading OS models with Transformers?
Dr. Arif demonstrates downloading TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat using Transformers library, using AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer, and apply_chat_template() method in his course.
What inference methods are taught in Generative and Agentic AI course?
Dr Arif's course covers inference using pipeline(), generate() method, and manual forward pass with AutoTokenizer and AutoModelForCausalLM for complete understanding.
How does Dr. Arif teach AI model selection?

Generative and Agentic AI course covers AI Benchmarks and LLM Leaderboards including Open LLM Leaderboard, Chatbot Arena, and factors like open vs closed source, model size, context window, and cost considerations.
What is the Chinchilla Scaling Law in Dr Arif's course?
Dr. Arif explains the Chinchilla Scaling Law in his Generative and Agentic AI course, teaching optimal model size and training data ratios for efficient LLM development.

HO 3.4 in Dr Arif's Information Security course includes Q5CSF21M, Q5DSF22M, Q5DSF22A, and Q5CSF23M as sessional instruments for Module 3.
What does HO 3.5 cover in Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 3.5 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Shell Codes including downloading from public repositories, generating with msfvenom and pwntools, writing custom assembly shell codes, and testing in C programs.
What is CA-05 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course?
CA-05 is the fifth continuous assessment in Dr Arif's Information Security course associated with shell codes and buffer overflow covered in HO 3.5.
What is covered in HO 3.6 of Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course?

HO 3.6 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers exploiting BoF vulnerable programs by injecting shell code to spawn local shell and exploiting echo server on Metasploitable2 for remote access from Kali.
What does HO 3.7 cover in the Information Security course?
HO 3.7 in Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course covers mitigation techniques for Buffer Overflow including developer-based, OS-based (DEP, ASLR), and compiler-based techniques (stack canary, PIC/PIE, CFP).
What are the main modules in Dr Arif's Information Security course?

Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course consists of Module 1 (Overview and Prerequisites), Module 2A (Network PenTesting), Module 2B (Web App PenTesting), Module 2C (Wireless PenTesting), Module 2D (Android App PenTesting), and Module 3 (Malware Analysis & Reverse Engineering).
What is covered in Module 2B of Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course?

Module 2B in Dr Arif's Information Security course covers Web Application PenTesting including OWASP Top10, DVWA, Burp suite, command injection, SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, and Social Engineering Toolkit as outlined in HO 2.9 and HO 2.10.
What is covered in Module 2C of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

Module 2C in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers Wireless Network PenTesting including Wi-Fi security protocols, 4-way handshake, deauth attacks, and password cracking as covered in HO 2.11.
What is covered in Module 2D of Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course?

Module 2D in Dr Arif's Information Security course covers Android App PenTesting including app development, reverse engineering, static analysis, OWASP Mobile Top10, DIVA app exploitation, and Metasploit for Android as covered in HO 2.12 through HO 2.15.
What is covered in Module 3 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

Module 3 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers Malware Analysis, Reverse Engineering, and Exploitation of C Binaries including buffer overflow, shell codes, and mitigation techniques as covered in HO 3.1 through HO 3.7.
What are the sessional instruments for Module 1 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course?
Module 1 in Dr Arif's Information Security course includes Q1DSF22M, Q1DSF22A, Q1CSF21M, and Q1CSF23M as sessional instruments associated with HO 1.4.

What CTF challenges are in Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course includes CTF 1 through CTF 5 as capture the flag challenges associated with reverse engineering covered in HO 3.3.
What is the first handout in Dr Arif's Information Security course?
The first handout in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course is HO 1.1 covering course information, CIA triad, and famous cyber-attacks in Module 1.
What is the last handout in Module 2 of Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course?

What is covered in HO 1.1 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 1.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers course information and protocols, CIA triad, threats to CIA, authentication vs authorization, cybersecurity categories, famous cyber-attacks, and course matrix.
What famous cyber-attacks are discussed in HO 1.1 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.1 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course discusses famous cyber-attacks including Morris, ILOVEYOU, Melissa virus, Stuxnet, Estonia attack, WannaCry, Lockbit, Pegasus, and DeepFakes.
What cybersecurity categories are covered in HO 1.1 of Dr Arif's course?

HO 1.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers categories including Information Security, Network Security, OS Security, Web-app Security, Mobile-app Security, Wi-Fi Security, Cloud Security, IoT Security, and LLM Security.
What virtual machines are installed in HO 1.2 of the Information Security course?
HO 1.2 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers installing Kali Linux, Windows10, Ubuntu Server, Metasploitable2, Metasploitable3, and Flare-VM using hypervisors or Docker containers.
What hypervisors are used in HO 1.2 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.2 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers using VirtualBox, UTM, VMware, Parallel Desktop, and MS Hyper-V for virtualization.
What OS concepts are recapped in HO 1.3 of Dr. Arif's Information Security course?
HO 1.3 in Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course covers versioning history of MS Windows, Linux, Mac OS, and Android, plus Linux file hierarchy, shell commands, and password storage.
What cryptography tools are covered in HO 1.3 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 1.3 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers Linux tools for encoding, hashing, encryption, digital signatures, and digital certificates.
What networking concepts are in HO 1.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 1.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers TCP/IP stack, ifconfig, ping, arp, route, DNS tools, xinetd services, bind vs reverse shells, and firewall configuration with iptables, nftables, and ufw.
What services are practiced in HO 1.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 1.4 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course includes hands-on practice with telnet, ssh, ftp, MySQL, Apache web server, and ngrok for external access.
What is Wireshark used for in HO 1.4 of the Information Security course?
HO 1.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course uses Wireshark for capturing and analyzing network traffic while accessing various services in the virtual lab.
What is covered in HO 2.1 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.1 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers the six phases of pentesting, ethical hacking, vulnerability types, exploit and payload, OWASP Top10, and cyber-attack types.
What anonymity concepts are in HO 2.1 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.1 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers different ways to achieve anonymity and the difference between Deep web and Dark web.
What reconnaissance tools are covered in HO 2.2 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 2.2 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers passive reconnaissance tools including host, nslookup, dig, whois, knockpy, netdiscover, traceroute, whatweb, theHarvester, sherlock, wfw00f, Google Dorking, and OSINT framework.
What scanning tools are in HO 2.3 of Dr. Arif's Information Security course?

What is CVE in HO 2.3 of the Cybersecurity course?

What is covered in HO 2.4 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.4 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Metasploit Framework, msfconsole anatomy, structure, and using auxiliary modules for scanning on Metasploitable2.
What services are exploited in HO 2.5 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

HO 2.5 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers exploiting services on Metasploitable2 including information disclosure, bindshell, samba, vsftpd, httpd, tomcat, UnrealIRCd, distccd, VNC, SMTP, PostgreSQL, plus EternalBlue on Windows10.
What brute force attacks are in HO 2.5 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.5 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers launching brute force attacks on ssh and telnet services running on Metasploitable2.
What payload tools are covered in HO 2.6 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.6 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers msfvenom and veil for generating payloads, plus VirusTotal for analyzing malicious programs.
What types of payloads are generated in HO 2.6 of the Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.6 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers generating payloads for new user creation, spawning local shell, bindshell, and reverse shell.
What privilege escalation techniques are in HO 2.7 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.7 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers common privilege escalation techniques and MSF post module for exploiting distccd and PostgreSQL on Metasploitable2.
What persistence mechanisms are covered in HO 2.8 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.8 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers maintaining access after exploiting NetBIOS on Metasploitable3 using EternalBlue exploit.
What covering tracks techniques are in HO 2.8 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.8 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers log files in Linux and Windows and common tools for clearing tracks.
What web app pentesting tools are covered in HO 2.9 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?

What vulnerable web apps are used in HO 2.9 of the Information Security course?

What injection attacks are covered in HO 2.10 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.10 in Dr. Arif's Information Security course covers command injection, SQL injection using SQLMap, XSS, CSRF, and SSRF vulnerabilities.
What frameworks are in HO 2.10 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.10 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Cybersecurity course covers Browser Exploitation Framework (BeEF) and Social Engineering Toolkit (SETOOLKIT).
What wireless security protocols are covered in HO 2.11 of the Information Security course?
HO 2.11 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers WEP, WPA, WPA2, and WPA3 security protocols and the 4-way handshake.
What wireless attack tools are in HO 2.11 of Dr Arif's Cybersecurity course?
HO 2.11 in Dr. Muhammad Arif Butt's Information Security course covers airmon-ng, airodump-ng, aireplay-ng for deauth attacks, and aircrack-ng, hashcat, John the Ripper for password cracking.
What Android development topics are in HO 2.12 of Dr Arif's Information Security course?
HO 2.12 in Dr. Arif's Cybersecurity course covers Android architecture, building Hello World app in Android Studio, app signing, and publishing on Google Play.
What Android pentesting tools are covered in HO 2.13 of the Information Security course?
